steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate y
steps.content:
The first equation has $5y$ and the second has $-5y$. Adding them cancels the $y$ terms immediately.

$$5y = 6x + 17$$$$-5y = 7x - 23$$
Adding both sides:
$$5y + (-5y) = (6x + 17) + (7x - 23)$$$$0 = 13x - 6$$$$13x = 6$$

steps.title: Multiply by 3 to find 39x
steps.content:
Since $13x = 6$, multiply both sides by 3:
$$39x = 18$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
